tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: GoogleSearch
tools.description: The tool can search the input query text from Google and return the related results.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Determine the imaging plane or view of the image?

dialogs.thought: Extract any on-image orientation markers or labels (e.g., R/L, A/P) that indicate the MRI plane.
Get a brief global description to infer whether the slice looks axial, sagittal, or coronal based on overall anatomy layout.

content.content: Grayscale MRI slice showing a symmetric cross-sectional anatomy with left and right soft tissues and muscle groups, and a central round airway-like structure; an arrow points to a lesion/mass near the midline. Orientation markers R and L appear on the sides.

arguments.query: MRI planes how to distinguish axial coronal sagittal by symmetry left right R L markers

dialogs.thought: Sanity-check typical visual cues for MRI planes (symmetry, airway appearance, R/L markers) to support the final classification.

content.content:
1) Radiopaedia: MRI planes (axial, coronal, sagittal) and how to recognize them by anatomy orientation.
2) MRIquestions: Imaging planes—axial/transverse vs coronal vs sagittal with common viewing conventions.
3) Kenhub/Anatomy resources: Cross-sectional (axial) anatomy shows bilateral symmetry and round airway/lumen.
4) University radiology teaching file: Recognizing axial neck MRI by paired muscles and central airway.
5) StatPearls/Imaging basics: R/L markers and slice appearance across standard planes.

arguments.attribute: presence and appearance of a central airway-like structure

dialogs.thought: Verify whether the image contains a central round airway structure consistent with a cross-sectional (axial) appearance.

content.content: The image contains a centrally located, roughly circular dark lumen surrounded by soft tissue, consistent with an airway or trachea seen in cross-section.

arguments.attribute: bilateral symmetry of lateral cervical soft tissues

dialogs.thought: Assess left–right symmetry of neck musculature to distinguish coronal from sagittal views.

content.content: Lateral soft tissues and muscle groups appear symmetrically distributed on both sides of the image, supporting a coronal imaging plane.

dialogs.content: The image shows a coronal MRI view of the cervical mass.
